data.title: Paramount Skydance may bid $22 to $24 a share for Warner Bros. Discovery - CNBC
data.description:
Paramount Skydance (NASDAQ:PSKY) may offer $22 to $24 a share for Warner Bros. Discovery (NASDAQ:WBD).

An offer may consist of 20%-30% in Paramount Skydance (NASDAQ:PSKY) stock and the remainder in cash, according to CNBC's David Faber, who cited speculation.

Shares of Warner Bros. Discovery rose 2.1%. 

The latest update come after CNBC's Faber said on Tuesday that a potential Paramount Skydance (NASDAQ:PSKY) bid for Warner Bros. Discovery (NASDAQ:WBD) may take a bit longer than expected.

data.title: Samsung's latest HBM product receives validation from Nvidia: report
data.description:
Samsung's (OTCPK:SSNLF) fifth-generation, 12-layer HBM3E has passed Nvidia's (NASDAQ:NVDA) qualification tests, meaning it can now become a critical supplier for Nvidia's most powerful artificial intelligence accelerators, according to The Korea Economic Daily.

Samsung's HBM3E had already passed qualification tests for Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) MI350, according to the report, which cited people familiar with the matter. It can now be used with Nvidia's B300 Blackwell Ultra GPU. Nvidia had already approved 12-layer HBM3E chips from SK Hynix and Micron Technology (MU).

However, after achieving this milestone, Samsung must now perfect HBM4, which is slated to appear in Nvidia's next generation of GPUs, the Vera Rubin. The Vera Rubin will succeed the Blackwell and is expected for release during the second half of 2026.

Samsung plans to send some HBM4 samples to Nvidia as soon as this month, the report said. The South Korean company is in talks to supply HBM4 to Broadcom (AVGO) and Google (GOOG)(GOOGL) as well. Samsung partnered with Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (TSM) to develop its HMB4 offering.

data.title: Johnson & Johnson reports positive results from multiple myeloma trial
data.description:
• Johnson & Johnson (NYSE:JNJ) announced promising results from a mid-stage trial evaluating a combination of Tecvayli (teclistamab) and Darzalex Faspro (daratumumab and hyaluronidase) for the treatment of multiple myeloma.
  • The study, which involved 49 patients placed in three groups, showed an overall response of 100% after the first round of treatment.
  • By the sixth treatment cycle, nearly 86% of patients had achieved a complete or very deep response, with no signs of detectable disease.
  • JNJ added that safety results confirmed the therapy was generally well tolerated.

data.title: KalVista, Ionis/Sobi among latest to win EU nod for rare disease drugs
data.description:
KalVista Pharmaceuticals (NASDAQ:KALV) and Ionis Pharmaceuticals (NASDAQ:IONS) announced on Friday that the European Commission has granted final approval for two of their treatments targeting rare genetic disorders.

Cambridge, Massachusetts-based KalVista (NASDAQ:KALV) said that the EC and Swiss medical regulator, the Swiss Agency for Therapeutic Products, approved its lead product, Ekterly, for hereditary angioedema, a rare disorder characterized by tissue swelling.

Specifically, the approvals allow the marketing of Ekterly as an oral on-demand treatment for acute attacks of HAE in patients aged 12 years and older.

Meanwhile, Ionis (NASDAQ:IONS) and its partner Sobi (OTCPK:BIOVF) (OTCPK:SWOBY) announced the EU approval of its anti-lipid agent Tryngolza as a treatment with diet for adults with familial chylomicronemia syndrome, a rare condition marked by high triglycerides.

The decision follows a positive opinion an expert panel of the EU drug regulator granted in July based on a Phase 3 Balance study, in which Tryngolza helped cut patients’ triglyceride levels with a statistically significant effect over six months.

data.title: Stock market opens in green a day after Wall Street notches record highs; eyes on Trump-Xi call
data.description:
Wall Street opened in the green on Friday, a day after the major averages closed at record highs, fueled by optimism from the Federal Reserve's rate cut. 

S&P 500 (SP500) +0.2%, the Dow (DJI) +0.2%, and the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) +0.4%.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) rose 2 basis points to 4.13%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) was flat at 3.58%.

On trade related news, President Donald Trump and Chinese President Xi Jinping have started their phone call, the Chinese Embassy in the U.S. said.

Wall Street's three major averages closed at record highs on Thursday, a day after the Federal Reserve cut interest rates by 25 basis points. Stocks were propelled by gains in big tech and cybersecurity.

"First, markets were still benefiting from the Fed’s announcements, where the dovish shift in the dot plot led to growing anticipation of further cuts ahead. Second, we had some stronger data on the labor market... so that helped to reassure investors that an economic slowdown would be avoided. And third, tech stocks did particularly well after Nvidia agreed to invest $5B in Intel," Deutsche Bank's Henry Allen said. 

Rate talks continued to be in focus. 

"That prospect of further rate cuts has propelled risk assets to new highs over recent sessions. Indeed, the Fed has now delivered 125 bps of rate cuts since September 2024, and both the dot plot and market pricing are pointing towards another two rate cuts by year-end. This is an interesting backdrop, as even with the 125 bps already delivered, you have to go back to the 1980s for the last time they cut that rapidly in a non-recessionary environment," Allen said. 

In other political news, the Trump administration asked the Supreme Court for an emergency order to fire Lisa Cook from the Federal Reserve Board of Governors, according to media reports.

"International investors have a keen interest in whether or not members of the Fed have more employment security than presidential counselors," UBS' Paul Donovan said.

data.title: Morgan Stanley to liquidate Calvert US Select Equity ETF next month
data.description:
The Calvert US Select Equity ETF (NYSEARCA:CVSE) is set to be liquidated on or about Oct. 20, 2025, Morgan Stanley Investment Management, part of Morgan Stanley (NYSE:MS), said on Friday.

The exchange-traded fund will no longer accept orders for the purchase of creation units effective upon the close of business on Oct. 14, 2025, which is also expected to be the CVSE's last full day of trading of NYSE Arca. 

Shareholders can sell their CVSE on NYSE Arca until market close on Oct. 14 or stay invested in the fund. 

Prior to the liquidation date, the fund will wind up its business and transition its assets to cash and cash equivalents in preparation for the liquidation and subsequent distribution of proceeds to remaining shareholders.

Launched and managed by Morgan Stanley IM, CVSE invests in growth and value stocks of large-cap U.S. companies that promote environmental responsibility.

data.title: Izotropic completes $375K private placement to support working capital
data.description:
• Izotropic Corp (OTCQB:IZOZF) completed a non-brokered private placement of 1.5M units at $0.25/unit, raising $375,000 in gross proceeds.
  • Each unit includes one common share and one transferable warrant.
  • Warrants are exercisable at $0.50/share for 3 years from the closing date.
  • Proceeds will be used for general working capital purposes.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: ZOOZ Power soars after shareholders approve financing to back Bitcoin strategy
data.description:
• ZOOZ Power (NASDAQ:ZOOZ) shareholders have approved all proposals related to the company’s previously announced $180 million private placement (PIPE) and Bitcoin treasury reserve strategy.
  • Key proposals approved include issuance of ordinary shares and warrants in connection with the offering, including $5 million sold in the initial private placement; amendment to the company’s Articles of Association to increase the authorized share capital; election of Alberto Franco and Jonas Grossman to the company’s board of directors upon the closing date. ZOOZ expects to close the PIPE during the week of Sept. 22, 2025.
  • Proceeds from the offering will primarily be used to launch ZOOZ’s Bitcoin treasury reserve strategy. The company intends to allocate around 95% of the net proceeds to purchase and hold Bitcoin on its balance sheet.
  • ZOOZ +199.5% premarket to $6.98
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: BlackRock favors gold, bitcoin, growth, and international stocks after Fed rate cut
data.description:
The Federal Reserve’s decision earlier this week to cut interest rates by 25 basis points marked the first reduction since December 2024, signaling a renewed phase of monetary easing. Investors and market participants are now assessing the implications of the move, with BlackRock, the world’s largest asset manager, offering a roadmap for where opportunities may emerge.

According to BlackRock, the rate cut strengthens the investment case for large-cap growth stocks, international equities, and alternative assets such as gold (XAUUSD:CUR) and bitcoin (BTC-USD). The firm highlighted that lower borrowing costs could support corporate earnings and valuations, particularly for growth-oriented companies. 

“We believe the U.S. economy will avoid recession for the foreseeable future; if that proves true, large-cap growth stocks are most likely to benefit from a lower rate environment,” BlackRock stated in a recent investor note. 

At the same time, the Federal Open Market Committee’s policy shift is expected to exert downward pressure on the U.S. dollar (DXY). A weaker currency “may further boost the appeal of international equities as well as portfolio diversifiers such as gold and bitcoin,” the investment institution added. 

With monetary policy entering a more accommodative phase, BlackRock suggests that investors remain flexible, as diversification may become increasingly valuable in the months ahead. 

Outlined below are a group of ETFs that fit the bill towards BlackRock’s viewpoint. 

Large-Cap Growth ETFs: (NYSEARCA:VUG), (NASDAQ:QQQ), (NYSEARCA:IVW), (NYSEARCA:SCHG), (NYSEARCA:MGK), (NASDAQ:IUSG), (NYSEARCA:VOOG), and (NYSEARCA:JGRO).

International ETFs: (NYSEARCA:VEA), (BATS:IEFA), (NYSEARCA:EFA), (NYSEARCA:SCHF), (NYSEARCA:SPDW), (NYSEARCA:IDEV), (NYSEARCA:FNDF), and (NYSEARCA:VSS).

Gold ETFs: (NYSEARCA:GLD), (NYSEARCA:IAU), (NYSEARCA:GLDM), (NYSEARCA:SGOL), (NYSEARCA:IAUM), (NYSEARCA:OUNZ), (BATS:AAAU), (NYSEARCA:BAR), VanEck Gold Miners ETF (NYSEARCA:GDX), VanEck Junior Gold Miners ETF (NYSEARCA:GDXJ), iShares MSCI Global Gold Miners ETF (NASDAQ:RING), and Direxion Daily Gold Miners Index Bull 2x Shares (NYSEARCA:NUGT).

Bitcoin ETFs: (NASDAQ:IBIT), (BATS:FBTC), (NYSEARCA:GBTC), (NYSEARCA:BTC), (BATS:ARKB), (NYSEARCA:BITO), and (BATS:HODL).

data.title: Indicators show consumer appetite for Apple's iPhone 17 stronger than expected; JP Morgan raises targets
data.description:
Indicators demonstrate consumers have a stronger-than-expected appetite for Apple's (NASDAQ:AAPL) new iPhone 17, which became generally available today.

Shares edged up 1% during early Friday trading.

These indicators prompted J.P. Morgan to increase its price target to $280 from $255 and maintain its Overweight rating. The firm also raised its revenue and earnings per share estimates.

The "early demand indications are outlining the opportunity for the volume cycle to not only track higher than our initial expectations (which incorporated concerns around digestion following a pull-forward in iPhone 16 series), but also upside to iPhone 16 series volumes on a y/y basis," said J.P. Morgan analysts, led by Samik Chatterjee, in a Friday investor note. "Led by the improved volume outlook, we are now forecasting iPhone volumes of 236 mn in FY26, which will represent a modest +2% y/y increase, which when combined with mix tailwinds will, in our view, support mid- to high-single digit iPhone revenue growth."

J.P. Morgan increased full fiscal 2026 adjusted EPS to $8.20 from $7.55 and full fiscal 2027 to $9.50 from $8.45. The investment firm increased its iPhone revenue estimate for fiscal 2026 to $223B from $200B and fiscal 2027 to $250B from $212B.

"As we look beyond FY26, consumer and investor excitement will shift to the foldable iPhone, which will feature another major form factor redesign/launch," Chatterjee said. "In addition to the product roadmap, FY27 will bring changes in relation to the product launch cadence with high-end iPhone 18 series launched in the fall heading into FY27, followed by the launch of base iPhone 18 series models in middle of FY27."

On Wednesday, Morgan Stanley also noted that "lead time analysis suggests that early demand for the iPhone 17 models is stronger than at the onset of the iPhone 16 cycle last year, a positive early indication that iPhone replacement cycles could be stabilizing and upgrade rates could be improving."

Companies that provide vital components for the iPhone 17 include Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (TSM), Samsung (OTCPK:SSNLF), Qualcomm (QCOM) and Corning (GLW).
